page: Microsoft Azure D96pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. The CPU configuration includes a 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96pls_v6 demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top 10%, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 464.93 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 205.97 GB/sec. It also achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations, database operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50% average category.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a compute-focused design that prioritizes raw core density over memory capacity. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, and web serving. Conversely, memory-intensive applications or single-threaded tasks may face resource limitations. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which should be factored into architectural planning. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded compilation, static web serving, database operations, and parallelized CPU-bound tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D96pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 464.93 GB/sec for large block reads. While single-core performance remains average, its high core density and strong multi-threaded capabilities make it highly cost-effective for parallel processing. It is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96pls_v6 is an ARM-based VM featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96pls_v6 is a 96-vCPU ARM virtual machine with 192 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 compute instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and optimized memory bandwidth for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz.
192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
No local storage or GPU resources included.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
